content.question: If Hungary abolished or substantially reformed the 6/8-year track admissions process, what magnitude of reduction in between-school segregation (if any) would be achievable without simultaneous reform of residential segregation, school choice policies, or other structural drivers identified as 'primary' in the literature?
content.reduction_path: Model segregation outcomes under counterfactual track-abolition scenarios using agent-based or microsimulation approaches; survey current Hungarian 6/8-year track students' residence patterns and choice processes to estimate how many segregating effects are mediated by track structure versus orthogonal structural factors; pilot small-scale changes to track admissions rules (e.g., lottery-based selection, demographic balancing requirements) and measure resulting segregation shifts before system-wide reform.
